verdict_reasoning: B4GALT1 is a type II single-pass TM glycosyltransferase canonically resident in the Golgi apparatus, where its N-terminal TM domain anchors it. However, B4GALT1 is well-documented to also appear on the cell surface (outer leaflet) under specific conditions: (1) In many cell types, a fraction cycles to the plasma membrane and is detectable by surface labeling. (2) In lactating mammary epithelium, it partners with alpha-lactalbumin to form lactose synthase, and the surface/secreted pool is biologically important. (3) Surface B4GALT1 has been shown to act as a signaling receptor for fibronectin and laminin on sperm and other cell types, functioning as a cell-surface lectin-like receptor — a documented non-Golgi surface pool. (4) A soluble secreted form exists. This dual Golgi/PM localization with documented surface biology in specific cell-type and activation contexts places it firmly in contextual/dual_localization rather than simply endomembrane_resident. The dominant compartment is Golgi, but the surface PM pool in specific cell types and states is well-established in the literature.
